title: Ground-state phase diagram of Rydberg atoms in a triangular-prism array
authors: Qing-Yuan Zuo, Shuo Geng, Shan-Wen Tsai, Jin Zhang
abstract: We study the ground-state phase diagram of Rydberg atoms in a triangular-prism optical tweezer array using the density matrix renormalization group. By tuning the detuning-to-Rabi-frequency ratio and the Rydberg blockade radius, the system realizes several density-wave phases with spontaneous breaking of translational and leg-exchange symmetries. Unlike two-leg Rydberg ladders with $\mathbb{Z}_2$ leg-exchange symmetry, the triangular prism has $\mathbb{D}_3$ symmetry, leading to a richer set of ordered phases and transitions. For blockade radius moderately larger than the lattice spacing, a phase with alternating double and single Rydberg occupancy appears at large detuning. It breaks $\mathbb{Z}_2$ translational and $\mathbb{Z}_3$ rotational symmetry while preserving a rung reflection symmetry. Upon decreasing detuning, it melts through two Berezinskii-Kosterlitz-Thouless transitions with an intermediate critical phase described by a $\mathbb{Z}_6$ clock model. At larger blockade radius, a phase with one Rydberg excitation per triangle and broken $\mathbb{D}_3$ symmetry appears through a first-order transition. When double occupation of neighboring triangles is suppressed, rung-trimerized density waves develop as detuning increases from the disordered phase. Their melting follows the same structure as in Rydberg chains and two-leg ladders: the $\mathbb{Z}_2$ case has Ising critical lines, while the $\mathbb{Z}_3$ and $\mathbb{Z}_4$ cases have chiral critical lines, with Potts and Ashkin-Teller points only on the corresponding commensurate lines. Inside the $\mathbb{Z}_2$ rung-trimerized phase, an entanglement-entropy peak signals a crossover regime with enhanced period-2 density modulation before a first-order transition into a $\mathbb{Z}_2\times\mathbb{D}_3$ phase. Floating phases with incommensurate quasi-long-range order appear between trimerized states of different periods.
summary: This paper uses DMRG to map the complex ground-state phase diagram of Rydberg atoms in a triangular-prism array. It reveals multiple density-wave phases characterized by spontaneous symmetry breaking, with transitions governed by distinct critical phenomena like BKT transitions and Ising/Potts universality classes. The study highlights how the geometry dictates the richness of the quantum phases accessible in these ultracold atomic systems.
main_problem: The study maps the ground-state phase diagram of Rydberg atoms confined in a triangular-prism optical tweezer array.
main_result: The system exhibits a rich variety of ordered phases, including density-wave states characterized by different symmetries ($Z_2 	imes D_3$, $Z_p$), and transitions between these phases are governed by various critical phenomena like BKT transitions and Ising/Potts universality classes.
method: The Density Matrix Renormalization Group (DMRG) algorithm is employed, supplemented by diagnostics like Von Neumann entanglement entropy and finite-size scaling analysis of correlation functions.
model_or_system: The system consists of Rydberg atoms in a triangular-prism lattice, modeled by a Hamiltonian incorporating Rabi coupling, detuning, and long-range Rydberg blockade interactions ($V_{r,r'}$).
key_observables: Von Neumann entanglement entropy ($S_{vN}$), connected density-density correlation function ($C(r)$), and order parameters ($\hat{M}_p$).
important_parameters: Detuning-to-Rabi-frequency ratio ($\Delta/\Omega$) and the Rydberg blockade radius ($R_b/a$).
main_assumptions: The analysis often relies on projecting the Hilbert space to an effective Hamiltonian, particularly in the large blockade radius limit.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the phase diagram in the $\Delta/\Omega - R_b/a$ plane, showing lobes corresponding to different crystalline orders, and plots of $S_{vN}$ and $C(r)$ used to locate critical points and crossovers.
paper_structure: The paper systematically explores the phase diagram by varying key parameters, analyzing transitions between different symmetry-broken phases ($Z_2 	imes Z_3 	o 	ext{Disordered}$ via BKT, $D_3 	o 	ext{Disordered}$ via first-order), and characterizing crossovers using entanglement entropy.
why_interesting: This work provides a detailed theoretical map of quantum phases in a highly tunable, geometrically constrained quantum simulator platform, linking specific symmetries ($D_3$) to complex critical behavior.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Ground-state phase diagram of the triangular-prism Rydberg atom array, obtained from the von Neumann entan- glement entropy SvN. The results are for L = 193 triangles with OBCs, and SvN is evaluated across the cut between the 96th and 97th triangles. We use the original Hamiltonian in Eq. (1) for Rb/a < 2.95, and the effective Hamiltonian in Eq. (2) for Rb/a ≥2.95. The dark lobes denote distinct crystalline orders, whose density patterns are shown in (d). The dashed lines in the disordered region indicate constant-k lines, where k is the oscillation wave vector in the short-range density-density correlations; the labels on these lines give k/2π. There is an extended k = π regime...

FIG. 2. Data collapse of the Binder cumulant U4 for the Ising transitions between the Z+ 2 phase and the disordered phase. Panels (a) and (b) are along the cuts Rb/a = 1.9 and 2.0, re- spectively, calculated from the original Hamiltonian (1). Pan- els (c) and (d) are for the same two cuts, but calculated from the effective Hamiltonian (2). The solid lines are polynomial fits to the collapsed data. The insets show U4 as a function of ∆/Ω. The DMRG truncation error is set to ε = 10−12.

FIG. 3. (a) Data collapse of the Binder cumulant U4 for the quantum phase transition between the Z+ 3 phase and the dis- ordered phase at the Potts CFT point. (b) Phase boundary of the Z+ 3 phase and the commensurate line with k = 2π/3 in the disordered phase. Solid circles denote transition points determined from U4 data collapse and are connected by spline interpolation. Diamonds denote extrapolated points at which the structure-factor peak is located at k = 2π/3 in the ther- modynamic limit. These points are connected by spline in- terpolation, and the resulting curve is extrapolated to larger ∆/Ωuntil it intersects the phase boundary at the Potts CFT point. (c) Data collapse of the...

FIG. 4. Same as Fig. 3, but for the phase transition between the Z+ 4 phase and the disordered phase. The commensurate line in (b) has wave vector k = π/2, and its intersection with the phase boundary determines the Ashkin-Teller CFT point. The DMRG truncation error is set to ε = 10−12 in (a) and ε = 10−10 in (c).

FIG. 6. Luttinger parameter K as a function of ∆/Ωfor vari- ous system sizes L, along the same cut as in Fig. 5, extracted from the crosscap overlaps under PBC. The same interaction truncation as in Fig. 5 is used. The curves of K intersect the two horizontal dashed lines at the critical values Kc = 1/18 and Kc = 1/8, which determine the two BKT transitions. The two insets show enlarged views near these two critical values using data for L = 24, 36, 48, 60, 72, 84, illustrating how the intersections with the critical values Kc evolve with sys- tem size. The bond dimension is set to D = 600.
